objective.subject: Mathematics
objective.topic: Angles, shape properties, line graphs and timetables
objective.statement: Reason from angle, polygon, 3-D representation, line-graph and timetable information, using properties, scale and units accurately.
misconceptions: Judges an angle by the length or direction of its arms instead of the amount of turn.
Classifies every symmetrical or familiar-looking polygon as regular without checking equal sides and equal angles.
Treats hidden edges in a 2-D drawing as absent or assumes one drawing proves an exact length.
Reads graph intervals as single units or combines timetable times as if they were decimal numbers.
objective.parent_explanation: Can use angle and shape properties and can read line graphs and timetables accurately, explaining answers with evidence and units.
objective.teacher_evidence: Stores convention reading, property or scale selection, calculation and justification separately so spatial, data-reading and arithmetic needs can be distinguished.

activities.title: I can read geometric and data representations, find what is known and justify what I conclude.
activities.prompt: Read the representation, model the relationship and justify your conclusion with a property, scale or unit.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Circle what the representation tells you exactly and mark what you must not assume.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Activate attention to labels, tick marks, hidden edges, axes, scales and units.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Use stated marks and values as evidence. A picture alone may not be drawn to scale.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Angle, polygon, cube drawing and line graph each show evidence and assumption columns.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Read the representation, model the relationship and justify your conclusion with a property, scale or unit.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce one transferable reasoning routine across geometry and statistics.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: First read the conventions. Next show the relationship. Finally state the evidence for your answer.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A three-panel READ-MODEL-JUSTIFY console links diagrams, calculations and explanation stems.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Find a missing angle on a straight line, then find the change between two points on a line graph.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model additive structure in two representations while keeping degrees and data units distinct.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Angles on a straight line total 180 degrees. Graph change is the difference between two correctly read values.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A 180-degree bar and a graph difference bar appear side by side with their own units.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Rotate through angle, shape, graph and timetable stations. Record the evidence before the answer.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Coordinate convention reading, property checking, scale interpretation and calculation without mixing domains.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: At every station, name the evidence and unit before you submit.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Four accessible station tabs preserve a common evidence-reason-answer table.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: The drawing looks regular and the graph point looks like 17. What must you check before agreeing?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Detect visual guessing from unmarked shapes and unexamined graph intervals.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Check equality marks for the polygon and calculate the value of one graph interval.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A regular-looking unmarked polygon and a non-unit graph scale receive evidence warning flags.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach your companion one rule for angles or shapes and one rule for graphs or timetables.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Gather explanation evidence across both curriculum areas.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Give an example, name the convention and explain one mistake the rule prevents.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The learner selects two evidence cards and records a spoken or typed explanation.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A polygon whose sides are all equal and whose angles are all equal.
vocabulary.audio_script: Regular means every side and every angle matches, not simply that the shape looks balanced.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Side and angle tick marks around a polygon.

vocabulary.child_explanation: An angle greater than 180 degrees but less than 360 degrees.
vocabulary.audio_script: A reflex angle is the larger turn beyond a straight angle and before a full turn.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A turn dial showing the larger arc.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The values represented by equal steps on an axis or diagram.
vocabulary.audio_script: Read the labelled values, then work out what each equal interval represents.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A line-graph axis with one interval bracketed and labelled.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A table showing when events start, finish or happen in sequence.
vocabulary.audio_script: Read along the correct row and column, then calculate elapsed time using hours and minutes.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A highlighted route through a departure-and-arrival table.

manipulative.purpose: Estimate, compare, draw and calculate angles while classifying polygons and interpreting 2-D representations of 3-D shapes.
manipulative.interaction_notes: Learners rotate rays by keyboard or number input, add equality marks, reveal hidden-edge conventions, choose nets or viewpoints and attach a property statement to every conclusion.
manipulative.accessibility_notes: All rotations accept typed degrees and step buttons; angle size is announced; diagrams have structured text descriptions; solid and dashed edges are named; no task depends on colour, dragging, fine motor control or spatial animation alone.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use a calm Orbit City analysis desk with one representation at a time, muted backgrounds and no automatic spinning solids or moving graph traces.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace ray rotation, solid turning and graph drawing with numbered static states, typed values and complete tables.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Announce representation type, labels, equality marks, scale and units before reading the question; provide replay without revealing the answer.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use short prompts, symbol glossaries, line focus and a separate evidence list so visual-spatial reasoning is not hidden by dense text.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Keep a READ MARKS-SHOW RELATIONSHIP-CALCULATE-JUSTIFY strip and hide unrelated station controls.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Remove timers, allow typed or step-button construction, preserve annotations and provide graph-table and diagram-text toggles.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Credit accurate evidence reading before calculation, allow not-enough-information answers and frame visual traps as representation checks.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach regular, irregular, acute, obtuse, reflex, scale, interval, duration, face, edge and vertex with labelled examples and sentence frames.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Estimate, compare, draw and calculate angles at a point and on a straight line.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: angle benchmark or total + labelled rays + estimate/draw/missing-angle task + degree input + property explanation
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Vary orientation and arm length independently; accept sensible estimation ranges; do not imply diagrams are to scale unless explicitly stated.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Classify regular and irregular polygons from explicit side and angle evidence.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: polygon image + optional equality marks/measurements + regularity claim + evidence selection + not-enough-information option
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include rotated, concave and familiar-looking distractors; never require classification from appearance where equality is not established.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Identify possible 3-D shapes from 2-D views, nets and face-edge-vertex evidence.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: labelled view/net + visible/hidden edge convention + candidate solids + property justification + ambiguity check
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Permit multiple possible solids when the view is insufficient; provide non-rotating labelled alternatives and verify every net geometrically.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Read non-unit scales and solve comparison, sum and difference problems from line graphs.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: single or paired series + labelled equal scale + table equivalent + read/compare/sum/difference prompt + unit response
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use honest axes, equal intervals and plausible data; avoid truncated-axis visual claims unless the task explicitly critiques them.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Read multi-row timetables and calculate waiting, journey and elapsed times across an hour.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: service/event timetable + named route + departure/arrival selection + clock-line duration + constraint or comparison
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Check chronological validity, 12/24-hour notation and accessible table headings; do not rely on culturally specific travel knowledge.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Judges an angle by the length or direction of its arms instead of the amount of turn.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner changes an angle classification when rays are lengthened, shortened or rotated without changing the turn.
repair_sequence: Place both angles on the same vertex and baseline.
Overlay a quarter-turn, straight-turn or degree arc.
Vary arm length and orientation while keeping the turn fixed.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner classifies and compares new angles independently of arm length and page orientation.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Classifies every symmetrical or familiar-looking polygon as regular without checking equal sides and equal angles.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner cites appearance, name or symmetry but cannot identify equality evidence.
repair_sequence: Mark or measure every side and angle claim.
Choose regular, irregular or not enough information from the evidence.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner justifies a fresh polygon classification using both side and angle evidence.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Treats hidden edges in a 2-D drawing as absent or assumes one drawing proves an exact length.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner undercounts edges or makes an unsupported metric claim from a perspective sketch.
repair_sequence: Separate visible edges from the solid's complete edge set.
Mark which claims are guaranteed, possible or not determinable.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner identifies a possible solid and states a limitation of the 2-D representation.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Reads graph intervals as single units or combines timetable times as if they were decimal numbers.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner counts spaces without deriving the scale or subtracts 14.35 - 13.48 as decimal hundredths.
repair_sequence: Bracket one graph interval or bridge the time to the next hour.
Recalculate and check against the graph table or clock line.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner accurately reads a new non-unit graph and a new elapsed-time item with units.

feedback.companion_prompt: Teach your companion one rule for angles or shapes and one rule for graphs or timetables.
feedback.parent_summary: Can use angle and shape properties and can read line graphs and timetables accurately, explaining answers with evidence and units.
feedback.teacher_evidence: Stores convention reading, property or scale selection, calculation and justification separately so spatial, data-reading and arithmetic needs can be distinguished.
next_actions: Route visual angle errors to invariant-turn overlays.
Route regularity and solid claims to guaranteed-possible-not-determinable evidence mapping.
Route graph and time errors to interval or bridge-hour repair.
Schedule mixed geometry and statistics retrieval at 1, 3, 7, 14 and 30 days.

body.prompt: Two adjacent angles make a straight line. One angle is 128 degrees. Find the other angle.

hints: Angles on a straight line total 180 degrees.
questions.explanation: 180 - 128 = 52, so the missing angle is 52 degrees.

body.prompt: A pentagon has five equal sides, but its angles are not all equal. Is it regular?
choices: No, because a regular polygon needs equal sides and equal angles
Yes, because equal sides are enough
Yes, because every pentagon is regular

expected_answer.value: No, because a regular polygon needs equal sides and equal angles

hints: Check both parts of the definition of regular.
What is known about the angles?
questions.explanation: The pentagon is irregular because regular polygons require all sides and all angles to be equal.

body.prompt: A line graph records 18 litres on Monday and 32 litres on Thursday. How much greater is Thursday's amount?

hints: Read each value using the scale of 2 litres per interval.
questions.explanation: Thursday's 32 litres is 14 litres greater than Monday's 18 litres.

body.prompt: A science show starts at 13:48 and ends at 14:35. How long does it last?

questions.explanation: From 13:48 to 14:00 is 12 minutes, and from 14:00 to 14:35 is 35 minutes; 12 + 35 = 47 minutes.

reward_payload.explanation: Can use angle and shape properties and can read line graphs and timetables accurately, explaining answers with evidence and units.
reward_payload.companion_prompt: Teach your companion one rule for angles or shapes and one rule for graphs or timetables.
